TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: told by Giɫgiɫaw of Vaɫulu village

S.FORM: aitsu a patjara penalisi izua maretimaɫimaɫi, ɫakua tjara liaw a mamamaw.
S.PHON: aiʦu a pacara pənaɭisi izua marətimaʎimaʎi, ʎakua cara ɭiaw a mamamaw.
S.FORM: aicu a patjara penalisi izua maretimaljimalji, ljakua tjara liaw a mamamaw.
S.PHON: aiʦu a paʦara pənaɭisi izua marətimaʎimaʎi, ʎakua ʦara ɭiaw a mamamaw.
S.TRANSL: As for taboos, there are different ones (in different villages), but certainly many are the same.

S.FORM: aitsu nu tjemugut, palisi a masi zaɫum a namasi vusam.
S.PHON: aiʦu nu cəmugut, paɭisi a masi zaʎum a namasi vusam.
S.FORM: aicu nu tjemugut, palisi a masi zaljum a namasi vusam.
S.PHON: aiʦu nu ʦəmugut, paɭisi a masi zaʎum a namasi vusam.
S.TRANSL: When we sow grain it is taboo for the person carrying the seed to carry water.

S.FORM: sika mapuqasaqasaw aya.
S.PHON: sika mapuqasaqasaw aja.
S.FORM: sika mapuqasaqasaw aya.
S.PHON: sika mapuqasaqasaw aja.
S.TRANSL: It is said to cause the ear to have no grain.

S.FORM: qau nu kivaqu anga palisi a kivinkatan aya.
S.PHON: qau nu kivaqu aŋa paɭisi a kivinkatan aja.
S.FORM: qau nu kivaqu anga palisi a kivinkatan aya.
S.PHON: qau nu kivaqu aŋa paɭisi a kivinkatan aja.
S.TRANSL: And when we are harvesting millet it is taboo to touch herbage in the forest(?).

S.FORM: sikisaqaɫaqaɫay aya.
S.PHON: sikisaqaʎaqaʎaj aja.
S.FORM: sikisaqaljaqaljay aya.
S.PHON: sikisaqaʎaqaʎaj aja.
S.TRANSL: It portends a plague (of rodents, etc.).

S.FORM: palisi a djemavats tua kakivaquin aya.
S.PHON: paɭisi a ɉəmavaʦ tua kakivaquin aja.
S.FORM: palisi a djemavac tua kakivaquin aya.
S.PHON: paɭisi a ɉəmavaʦ tua kakivaquin aja.
S.TRANSL: It is taboo to walk through a millet field being harvested, they say.

S.FORM: inika sikaɫadjeɫadjek aya.
S.PHON: inika sikaʎaɉəʎaɉək aja.
S.FORM: inika sikaljadjeljadjek aya.
S.PHON: inika sikaʎaɉəʎaɉək aja.
S.TRANSL: It won’t have dew (and won’t be a good harvest).

S.FORM: palisi a masi pariuk nu namasi vusam itjen.
S.PHON: paɭisi a masi pariuk nu namasi vusam icən.
S.FORM: palisi a masi pariuk nu namasi vusam itjen.
S.PHON: paɭisi a masi pariuk nu namasi vusam iʦən.
S.TRANSL: When we are carrying seed it is taboo to carry a wok.

S.FORM: sika mapuqiduqidung aya.
S.PHON: sika mapuqiduqiduŋ aja.
S.FORM: sika mapuqiduqidung aya.
S.PHON: sika mapuqiduqiduŋ aja.
S.TRANSL: They say the grain will go black like charcoal.

S.FORM: nu uri tjemugut itjen, palisi a kiqaqivuan tua tjuḍalian.
S.PHON: nu uri cəmugut icən, paɭisi a kiqaqivuan tua cuɖaɭian.
S.FORM: nu uri tjemugut itjen, palisi a kiqaqivuan tua tjudralian.
S.PHON: nu uri ʦəmugut iʦən, paɭisi a kiqaqivuan tua ʦuɖaɭian.
S.TRANSL: When we are going to sow grain it is taboo to talk to people expecting a baby.

S.FORM: nu palisilisi itjen palisi a pakisapuy, ayatua nasi aitsu a sapuy nu misepi itjen.
S.PHON: nu paɭisiɭisi icən paɭisi a pakisapuj, ajatua nasi aiʦu a sapuj nu misəpi icən.
S.FORM: nu palisilisi itjen palisi a pakisapuy, ayatua nasi aicu a sapuy nu misepi itjen.
S.PHON: nu paɭisiɭisi iʦən paɭisi a pakisapuj, ajatua nasi aiʦu a sapuj nu misəpi iʦən.
S.TRANSL: When we are observing religious rites it is taboo to give someone a light, because fire represents breath/life when we dream.

S.FORM: nu venuɫuq itjen a pasa vukivukid, vuɫuqen itjen nua tsemas aya.
S.PHON: nu vənuʎuq icən a pasa vukivukid, vuʎuqən icən nua ʦəmas aja.
S.FORM: nu venuljuq itjen a pasa vukivukid, vuljuqen itjen nua cemas aya.
S.PHON: nu vənuʎuq iʦən a pasa vukivukid, vuʎuqən iʦən nua ʦəmas aja.
S.TRANSL: When we throw something towards the woods, the spirits throw things at us, we are told.

S.FORM: nu keman itjen i vukivukid, nu i dedet itjen tua vukivukid, palisi a ika padjalian.
S.PHON: nu kəman icən i vukivukid, nu i dədət icən tua vukivukid, paɭisi a ika paɉaɭian.
S.FORM: nu keman itjen i vukivukid, nu i dedet itjen tua vukivukid, palisi a ika padjalian.
S.PHON: nu kəman iʦən i vukivukid, nu i dədət iʦən tua vukivukid, paɭisi a ika paɉaɭian.
S.TRANSL: When we eat in the woods, when we are near the woods, it is taboo not to give an offering to the spirits.

S.FORM: kilinguɫan itjen.
S.PHON: kiɭiŋuʎan icən.
S.FORM: kilinguljan itjen.
S.PHON: kiɭiŋuʎan iʦən.
S.TRANSL: They will sit and eat with us.

S.FORM: nu sekuliɫan itjen nu kemakan itjen, tjara izua matsay a matsidiɫ aya.
S.PHON: nu səkuɭiʎan icən nu kəmakan icən, cara izua maʦaj a maʦidiʎ aja.
S.FORM: nu sekuliljan itjen nu kemakan itjen, tjara izua macay a macidilj aya.
S.PHON: nu səkuɭiʎan iʦən nu kəmakan iʦən, ʦara izua maʦaj a maʦidiʎ aja.
S.TRANSL: If we are ‘tripped’ (by a bird calling(?) to us) when we are eating, it is said that someone will certainly die.

S.FORM: palisi a vaqesingan nu sema vavua itjen, nu uri sema tua namatsay itjen.
S.PHON: paɭisi a vaqəsiŋan nu səma vavua icən, nu uri səma tua namaʦaj icən.
S.FORM: palisi a vaqesingan nu sema vavua itjen, nu uri sema tua namacay itjen.
S.PHON: paɭisi a vaqəsiŋan nu səma vavua iʦən, nu uri səma tua namaʦaj iʦən.
S.TRANSL: It is taboo to sneeze when we are going to the fields or when we are going to where someone has died.

S.FORM: tja sikasaqetjuqetju aya.
S.PHON: ca sikasaqəcuqəcu aja.
S.FORM: tja sikasaqetjuqetju aya.
S.PHON: ʦa sikasaqəʦuqəʦu aja.
S.TRANSL: It is said it will mean sickness.

S.FORM: nu kaquɫisan itjen, inika sitsiketsikel nu sema dedet itjen, nu ika tjen a mavunglay.
S.PHON: nu kaquʎisan icən, inika siʦikəʦikəɭ nu səma dədət icən, nu ika cən a mavuŋɭaj.
S.FORM: nu kaquljisan itjen, inika sicikecikel nu sema dedet itjen, nu ika tjen a mavunglay.
S.PHON: nu kaquʎisan iʦən, inika siʦikəʦikəɭ nu səma dədət iʦən, nu ika ʦən a mavuŋɭaj.
S.TRANSL: When things are the wrong way round (when the kirkir sings before the tsugaw bird), it doesn’t cause us to go back if we are just going a short distance; or if we aren’t expecting a child.

S.FORM: ɫakua a mavunglay katua uri sema tsadja a uri kiqetsi, palisi aravats.
S.PHON: ʎakua a mavuŋɭaj katua uri səma ʦaɉa a uri kiqəʦi, paɭisi aravaʦ.
S.FORM: ljakua a mavunglay katua uri sema cadja a uri kiqeci, palisi aravac.
S.PHON: ʎakua a mavuŋɭaj katua uri səma ʦaɉa a uri kiqəʦi, paɭisi aravaʦ.
S.TRANSL: But if we are expecting a child or going some distance to fight, it is a serious taboo.

S.FORM: tja sika tjaparuparut.
S.PHON: ca sika caparuparut.
S.FORM: tja sika tjaparuparut.
S.PHON: ʦa sika ʦaparuparut.
S.TRANSL: We will come off worst.

S.FORM: nu saqetju itjen, matu qinuɫis itjen tua tja siselapay aya.
S.PHON: nu saqəcu icən, matu qinuʎis icən tua ca sisəɭapaj aja.
S.FORM: nu saqetju itjen, matu qinuljis itjen tua tja siselapay aya.
S.PHON: nu saqəʦu iʦən, matu qinuʎis iʦən tua ʦa sisəɭapaj aja.
S.TRANSL: If we are ill, our suffering will be severe, as if we were turned inside out.

S.FORM: palisi uta a patsugavan nu semainu anga itjen.
S.PHON: paɭisi uta a paʦugavan nu səmainu aŋa icən.
S.FORM: palisi uta a pacugavan nu semainu anga itjen.
S.PHON: paɭisi uta a paʦugavan nu səmainu aŋa iʦən.
S.TRANSL: It is a bad omen if the tsugaw bird sings wherever we are going.

S.FORM: tja sika luɫaluɫay, tja sika malaylaylay nu saqetju itjen.
S.PHON: ca sika ɭuʎaɭuʎaj, ca sika maɭajɭajɭaj nu saqəcu icən.
S.FORM: tja sika luljaluljay, tja sika malaylaylay nu saqetju itjen.
S.PHON: ʦa sika ɭuʎaɭuʎaj, ʦa sika maɭajɭajɭaj nu saqəʦu iʦən.
S.TRANSL: We will be tired. If we are ill, our illness will be long drawn out.

S.FORM: palisi uta a pakeɫisan.
S.PHON: paɭisi uta a pakəʎisan.
S.FORM: palisi uta a pakeljisan.
S.PHON: paɭisi uta a pakəʎisan.
S.TRANSL: It is a bad omen if the keɫis bird sings.

S.FORM: tja sipatsatsay i djalan, tja sipagerigerits tua nemanemanga aya.
S.PHON: ca sipaʦaʦaj i ɉaɭan, ca sipagərigəriʦ tua nəmanəmaŋa aja.
S.FORM: tja sipacacay i djalan, tja sipagerigeric tua nemanemanga aya.
S.PHON: ʦa sipaʦaʦaj i ɉaɭan, ʦa sipagərigəriʦ tua nəmanəmaŋa aja.
S.TRANSL: They say someone will die on the track, or we will have some grief to scream about.

S.FORM: nu alapen a tja itung nua qayaqayam, palisi aravats.
S.PHON: nu aɭapən a ca ituŋ nua qajaqajam, paɭisi aravaʦ.
S.FORM: nu alapen a tja itung nua qayaqayam, palisi aravac.
S.PHON: nu aɭapən a ʦa ituŋ nua qajaqajam, paɭisi aravaʦ.
S.TRANSL: If our clothes are taken by a bird, that is a very bad omen.

S.FORM: sipatsatsay ta qaɫa aya.
S.PHON: sipaʦaʦaj ta qaʎa aja.
S.FORM: sipacacay ta qalja aya.
S.PHON: sipaʦaʦaj ta qaʎa aja.
S.TRANSL: They say we will be killed by our enemies.

S.FORM: palisi a qinuɫivanglaw a qadaw.
S.PHON: paɭisi a qinuʎivaŋɭaw a qadaw.
S.FORM: palisi a qinuljivanglaw a qadaw.
S.PHON: paɭisi a qinuʎivaŋɭaw a qadaw.
S.TRANSL: It is a bad omen when there is a halo round the sun (when the sun is “rainbow-ed”).

S.FORM: sipatsatsay nua mamazangiɫan aya.
S.PHON: sipaʦaʦaj nua mamazaŋiʎan aja.
S.FORM: sipacacay nua mamazangiljan aya.
S.PHON: sipaʦaʦaj nua mamazaŋiʎan aja.
S.TRANSL: It means a chieftain will die.

S.FORM: aitsu nu sema tjai viri tua djalan a sisiɫ a qatjuvi, qemtseng aya, saka palisi a puarut a sema vavua a sema tjuqaɫa.
S.PHON: aiʦu nu səma cai viri tua ɉaɭan a sisiʎ a qacuvi, qəmʦəŋ aja, saka paɭisi a puarut a səma vavua a səma cuqaʎa.
S.FORM: aicu nu sema tjai viri tua djalan a sisilj a qatjuvi, qemceng aya, saka palisi a puarut a sema vavua a sema tjuqalja.
S.PHON: aiʦu nu səma ʦai viri tua ɉaɭan a sisiʎ a qaʦuvi, qəmʦəŋ aja, saka paɭisi a puarut a səma vavua a səma ʦuqaʎa.
S.TRANSL: If a quaker-thrush or a snake goes across the track from right to left, it is said to be blocked; and it is taboo to go straight to the fields or to another village.

S.FORM: ɫakua nu tja teqitsen azua qatjuvi, sa tja pasa tjai viri, inika palisi.
S.PHON: ʎakua nu ca təqiʦən azua qacuvi, sa ca pasa cai viri, inika paɭisi.
S.FORM: ljakua nu tja teqicen azua qatjuvi, sa tja pasa tjai viri, inika palisi.
S.PHON: ʎakua nu ʦa təqiʦən azua qaʦuvi, sa ʦa pasa ʦai viri, inika paɭisi.
S.TRANSL: But if we use a stick to flip the snake over to the right and we pass it to the left it is not taboo.

S.FORM: aitsu a qayaqayam nu tja vuɫuqen a pasa navaɫ saka sema tjai navaɫ, inika palisi uta.
S.PHON: aiʦu a qajaqajam nu ca vuʎuqən a pasa navaʎ saka səma cai navaʎ, inika paɭisi uta.
S.FORM: aicu a qayaqayam nu tja vuljuqen a pasa navalj saka sema tjai navalj, inika palisi uta.
S.PHON: aiʦu a qajaqajam nu ʦa vuʎuqən a pasa navaʎ saka səma ʦai navaʎ, inika paɭisi uta.
S.TRANSL: As for the birds, if we throw things to make them go to the right and they go to the right, it isn’t taboo to go on.

S.FORM: palisi a lemaketsuɫ tua mamazangiɫan.
S.PHON: paɭisi a ɭəmakəʦuʎ tua mamazaŋiʎan.
S.FORM: palisi a lemakeculj tua mamazangiljan.
S.PHON: paɭisi a ɭəmakəʦuʎ tua mamazaŋiʎan.
S.TRANSL: It is taboo to walk over the limb of a chieftain.

S.FORM: sikakalakala aya.
S.PHON: sikakaɭakaɭa aja.
S.FORM: sikakalakala aya.
S.PHON: sikakaɭakaɭa aja.
S.TRANSL: It is said to cause arthritis.

S.FORM: palisi petsungu tua tsuqu a kasiw.
S.PHON: paɭisi pəʦuŋu tua ʦuqu a kasiw.
S.FORM: palisi pecungu tua cuqu a kasiw.
S.PHON: paɭisi pəʦuŋu tua ʦuqu a kasiw.
S.TRANSL: It is taboo to use the bischoffia tree for firewood.

S.FORM: sikaḍaquḍaquɫ.
S.PHON: sikaɖaquɖaquʎ.
S.FORM: sikadraqudraqulj.
S.PHON: sikaɖaquɖaquʎ.
S.TRANSL: It causes goitre.

S.FORM: palisi a tsemuɫu a mavunglay tua qatsang.
S.PHON: paɭisi a ʦəmuʎu a mavuŋɭaj tua qaʦaŋ.
S.FORM: palisi a cemulju a mavunglay tua qacang.
S.PHON: paɭisi a ʦəmuʎu a mavuŋɭaj tua qaʦaŋ.
S.TRANSL: It is taboo for someone expecting a baby to butcher a pig.

S.FORM: sikamapuqatsaqatsang aya.
S.PHON: sikamapuqaʦaqaʦaŋ aja.
S.FORM: sikamapuqacaqacang aya.
S.PHON: sikamapuqaʦaqaʦaŋ aja.
S.TRANSL: They say it causes epilepsy.

S.FORM: palisi a qemtsi tua qemuzimuzip anga.
S.PHON: paɭisi a qəmʦi tua qəmuzimuzip aŋa.
S.FORM: palisi a qemci tua qemuzimuzip anga.
S.PHON: paɭisi a qəmʦi tua qəmuzimuzip aŋa.
S.TRANSL: It is also taboo for them to kill any animal.

S.FORM: sipatsatsay aya.
S.PHON: sipaʦaʦaj aja.
S.FORM: sipacacay aya.
S.PHON: sipaʦaʦaj aja.
S.TRANSL: They say it will cause a death.

S.FORM: palisi a semenay nu tsaviɫ anga tua puaɫak.
S.PHON: paɭisi a səmənaj nu ʦaviʎ aŋa tua puaʎak.
S.FORM: palisi a semenay nu cavilj anga tua pualjak.
S.PHON: paɭisi a səmənaj nu ʦaviʎ aŋa tua puaʎak.
S.TRANSL: It is taboo to sing a year after a birth.

S.FORM: siqauqaung aya.
S.PHON: siqauqauŋ aja.
S.FORM: siqauqaung aya.
S.PHON: siqauqauŋ aja.
S.TRANSL: They say the child will be a cry-baby.

S.FORM: palisi a kemzekez a venengetj uta.
S.PHON: paɭisi a kəmzəkəz a vənəŋəc uta.
S.FORM: palisi a kemzekez a venengetj uta.
S.PHON: paɭisi a kəmzəkəz a vənəŋəʦ uta.
S.TRANSL: It is taboo for someone expecting a baby to nail or tie something.

S.FORM: sikapazangazangal nu puaɫak aya.
S.PHON: sikapazaŋazaŋaɭ nu puaʎak aja.
S.FORM: sikapazangazangal nu pualjak aya.
S.PHON: sikapazaŋazaŋaɭ nu puaʎak aja.
S.TRANSL: They say it will lead to a difficult birth.
